title: Adirondack-Style Outdoor Coffee Table
summary: A simple 2x4-and-1x4 coffee table sized to match a set of Adirondack chairs — apron box on straight legs, planked top, all straight cuts.
description:
This coffee table is deliberately the simplest piece in an Adirondack seating set: an apron box built from 2x4s, straight (not splayed) legs clamped and screwed to the corners, and a 1x4 planked top nailed down from below. No angled cuts anywhere in the base makes this a genuinely approachable build for pairing with a set of Adirondack chairs without needing the chair's own compound-angle joinery.

Check the apron box for square by measuring corner to corner on both diagonals and adjusting until the two measurements match — the plan calls this out explicitly rather than trusting a framing square alone, since a small box like this is quick to check both ways. Clamping the legs to the frame before drilling is what keeps them from shifting while you predrill the mounting holes, and predrilling in a deliberate pattern (rather than a straight line) is a small detail that reads as considered rather than accidental on the finished piece.

The top boards attach with a countersink-drilled hole 2-1/4" in from each end, driven through from below with exterior screws and glue — or galvanized finish nails and glue as a no-countersink alternative. Either way, this is a fast weekend build with a short, simple tool list.

materials.name: Exterior deck screws, 2", or galvanized finish nails

cutList.note: One is an additional center support.

steps.title: Build the apron box
steps.body: Attach the side aprons to the end aprons with 2-1/2" exterior screws through predrilled countersunk holes. Check square by measuring diagonally corner to corner and adjusting until both diagonals match. Add the center support apron across the middle.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Clamp each leg to the frame and predrill four holes in a deliberate pattern through the apron into the leg, then attach with exterior screws.

steps.title: Add the top and finish
steps.body: Drill two countersunk holes on each end of every tabletop board, 2-1/4" in from the ends. Attach through the predrilled holes with exterior deck screws and glue, or galvanized finish nails and glue. Sand and finish.
materials: Exterior deck screws, 2", or galvanized finish nails

unresolvedImages.alt: Simple outdoor coffee table matching an Adirondack chair set
